rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical 1,2-difluoroalkylation/cyclization of N-methacryloyl-2-arylbenzimidazoles (and 2-arylindoles) with sodium difluoroalkylsulfinates to give CF2Me-substituted benzimidazo/indolo[2,1-a]isoquinolinones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of MeCF2SO2Na to a sulfonyl radical (which loses SO2 to give the MeCF2 radical) initiates the cascade; the anode also oxidizes the post-cyclization aryl/cyclohexadienyl radical B to aryl cation C. Cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: MeCF2SO2 sulfonyl radical (from anodic SET of sulfinate)
Carbon-centered radical A (after MeCF2 addition to acrylamide alkene)
Cyclohexadienyl/aryl radical B (after intramolecular cyclization onto arene)
Aryl cation C (after second anodic oxidation of B)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes MeCF2SO2– to its sulfonyl radical, which loses SO2 to give the MeCF2 radical; this adds to the acrylamide C=C to form C-radical A, which cyclizes intramolecularly onto the 2-aryl ring to give aryl radical B; B is oxidized at the anode to aryl cation C, which deprotonates (rearomatizes) to product 3. Cathodic reduction of H+ gives H2.

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Product formation fully suppressed; BHT–CF2Me adduct detected by HRMS, indicating a free MeCF2 radical.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product yield dropped to 16%; DPE–CF2Me radical adduct detected by HRMS, supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Substrates 1a and 2a (CV experiment)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks at 1.47 V (1a) and 0.67 V (2a) vs Ag/AgNO3, indicating sulfinate 2a is more easily oxidized than 1a, consistent with anodic initiation at the sulfinate.

probe_reagents.name: No electricity control
probe_reagents.observation: No product detected, confirming the reaction is electrochemically driven.

notes: X-ray structures of 3m (CCDC 2303467) and 5i (CCDC 2307007) confirm product connectivity. Mechanism is proposed as a radical cyclization with terminal anodic oxidation/deprotonation (radical–cation crossover at the arene) rather than HAT-based rearomatization; cathode serves only for proton reduction. Scope tolerates halides, esters, nitriles, methoxy, naphthyl and thienyl arenes, and extends to cyclopropyl-CF2 sulfinate.
